title: A Farewell by Charles Kingsley — Inspirational Poetry Lines
content:
I

My fairest child, I have no song to give you;
No lark could pipe to skies so dull and grey:
Yet, ere we part, one lesson I can leave you
For every day.

II

Be good, sweet maid, and let who will be clever;
Do noble things, not dream them, all day long:
And so make life, death, and that vast for-ever
One grand, sweet song.
meaning: "A Farewell" by Charles Kingsley is a inspirational poem consisting of 13 lines. The poet explores themes of inspirational through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "I ...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Charles Kingsley's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
